name: Saltimbocca Alla Romana
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup White Frascati or other dry white wine
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the veal into 8 slices. Place the slices between sheets of wax paper and pound thin.
2. Sprinkle one side of each slice with salt, pepper, and a small amount of flour.
3. Place half of the veal slices on a working board, floured sides down. Spoon about 2 tablespoons butter over them and sprinkle lightly with more salt and pepper.
4. Layer 1 slice of Proscuitto and 1 slice of Parmesan cheese on each veal slice. Add a sage leaf.
5. Mix minced garlic and parsley and divide the mixture among the veal slices.
6. Arrange shaved truffles on top, adding more if desired.
7. Spoon remaining butter over the veal, then add the floured sides up.
8. Press edges together with a knife handle.
9. In a skillet, melt the reserved butter. Place the bundles and sauté for 5 minutes until browned, turning and cooking an additional 3 minutes.
10. Pour in Frascati wine and shake the skillet to baste; cover and cook for another 5 minutes.
11. Dice remaining Proscuitto and add with the reserved tablespoon of butter to a small pan. Heat until warm.
12. Season with salt and pepper, then transfer Saltimbocca to plates. Spoon sauce over and serve with peas. Enjoy the Frascati wine.
NOTE: If using dried sage, soak in warm water briefly before pressing dry. Avoid ground sage for this dish.
